The **2022 [European Figure Skating Championships](/wiki/European_Figure_Skating_Championships "European Figure Skating Championships")** were held from 10 to 16 January 2022 at the [Tondiraba Ice Hall](/wiki/Tondiraba_Ice_Hall "Tondiraba Ice Hall") in [Tallinn, Estonia](/wiki/Tallinn,_Estonia "Tallinn, Estonia").[[1]](#cite_note-announcement-1) Medals were awarded in [men's singles, women's singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and [ice dance](/wiki/Ice_dance "Ice dance"). The competition determined the entry quotas for each federation at the [2023 European Championships](/wiki/2023_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2023 European Figure Skating Championships"). Tallinn was announced as the host in June 2019.[[2]](#cite_note-ISU190615-2) The city had previously hosted the competition in [2010](/wiki/2010_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2010 European Figure Skating Championships").

## Qualification

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Qualification")]

### Age and minimum TES requirements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Age and minimum TES requirements")]

The competition was open to skaters from all European [member nations](/wiki/International_figure_skating "International figure skating") of the [International Skating Union](/wiki/International_Skating_Union "International Skating Union"). The corresponding competition for non-European skaters was the [2022 Four Continents Championships](/wiki/2022_Four_Continents_Figure_Skating_Championships "2022 Four Continents Figure Skating Championships").

Skaters were eligible if they turned 15 years of age before 1 July 2021 and met the minimum technical elements score requirements. The ISU accepted scores if they were obtained at senior-level ISU-recognized international competitions during the ongoing season at least 21 days before the first official practice day of the championships or during the two preceding seasons.[[1]](#cite_note-announcement-1)

Minimum technical scores

| Discipline | [SP](/wiki/Short_program_(figure_skating) "Short program (figure skating)") / [RD](/wiki/Rhythm_dance "Rhythm dance") | [FS](/wiki/Free_skating "Free skating") / [FD](/wiki/Free_dance_(ice_dance) "Free dance (ice dance)") |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Men | 28 | 46 |
| Women | 23 | 40 |
| Pairs | 25 | 42 |
| Ice dance | 28 | 44 |

* SP/RD and FS/FD scores may be attained at different events.

### Number of entries per discipline

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2022_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Number of entries per discipline")]

Normally, the number of entries would be based on the results of the preceding European Championships. However, as the [2021 European Championships](/wiki/2021_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2021 European Figure Skating Championships") had been cancelled, the results of the [2020 European Championships](/wiki/2020_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2020 European Figure Skating Championships") were used instead.[[3]](#cite_note-n_entries-3)
